    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: HotKey.SetKeyUp
functions.description: Whether to trigger script both on key down and key up. The script parameter will have two values. First is the hotkey ID and second KeyDown or KeyUp as text.

parameters.name: HotKeyRef

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: Pass 1 to receive key down and up events. Pass 0 to only get one event for key down.

functions.name: HotKey.SetScript
functions.description: Sets which script to call if hotkey is pressed. Use HotKey.GetTag to query a tag value if needed. Default parameter passed to the script is the HotKey reference number. On macOS we include a second parameter to indicate whether it is KeyDown or KeyUp (see HotKey.SetKeyUp ). With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: HotKeyRef
parameters.description: The reference value for the hotkey as returned by HotKey.Register function.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description: The parameter to pass. If empty or not defined, we pass the HotKey reference number.

examples.label: Register Control-F5:
examples.text_code:
Set Field [
HotKeys::HotKey
ID;
MBS
("
HotKey.Register
"; "F5"; "control")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("HotKey.SetScript";
HotKeys::HotKey
ID; Get(FileName); "HotkeyAction")]

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("Hotkey.Register"; "F5"; "control")</Calculation>
    <Field table="HotKeys" id="99999" name="HotKey ID"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("HotKey.SetScript"; HotKeys::HotKey ID; Get(FileName); "HotkeyAction")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("MarkDown.Release"; $r) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Convert in Let():

Let ( [ pTextMitMarkdown = "This is a multiline ¶**Text**¶¶new paragraph" ; vMarkdown = MBS (" MarkDown.Create "; pTextMitMarkdown ) ; vHTML = MBS ("MarkDown.HTML"; vMarkdown ) ; vRelease = MBS (" MarkDown.Release "; vMarkdown ) ]; vHTML) Example result:

This is a multiline Text

new paragraph

[
 pTextMitMarkdown = "This is a multiline ¶**Text**¶¶new paragraph" ;
 vMarkdown =MBS("MarkDown.Create"; pTextMitMarkdown ) ;
 vHTML = MBS("MarkDown.HTML"; vMarkdown ) ;
 vRelease = MBS("MarkDown.Release"; vMarkdown )

];

vHTML)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MetaDataQuery.AttributesForFile
functions.description: Queries properties for a file. Returns JSON block with attributes. This provides information Spotlight knows about the file. Includes a lot of metadata like values from EXIF and IPTC. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the file to check.

examples.label: Query download URL of file:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$path
; Value: "/Users/cs/Downloads/test.html" ]
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "MetaDataQuery.AttributesForFile";
$path
) ]
Set Variable [
$link
; Value:
MBS
("
JSON.GetPathItem
";
$json
; "kMDItemWhereFroms¶0") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Link" ;
$link
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"/Users/cs/Downloads/test.html" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MetaDataQuery.AttributesForFile"; $path) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetPathItem"; $json; "kMDItemWhereFroms¶0") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$link</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Link" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$link </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MetaDataQuery.Create
functions.description: Creates a new metadata search object. Please call later MetaDataQuery.Close to free the object.

functions.name: MetaDataQuery.GetEvaluate
functions.description: Queries expression to be run when shell is done.

parameters.name: ShellRef
parameters.description: The reference number from the shell.

functions.name: MetaDataQuery.GetScriptFileName
functions.description: Queries filename of script to call in when shell is done.

parameters.name: ShellRef
parameters.description: The reference number from the shell.

functions.name: MetaDataQuery.GetScriptName
functions.description: Queries name of script to call when shell is done.

parameters.name: ShellRef
parameters.description: The reference number from the shell.

functions.name: MetaDataQuery.IsGathering
functions.description: Query whether gathering. A Boolean value that indicates whether the receiver is in the initial gathering phase of the query.

parameters.name: Query
parameters.description: The reference number of the query.

examples.label: Wait for the search to finish:
examples.text_code:
Loop
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,2 ]
Exit Loop If [
MBS
( "MetaDataQuery.IsGathering";
$Query
) = 0 ]
End Loop
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,2 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> MBS( "MetaDataQuery.IsGathering"; $Query ) = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MetaDataQuery.IsStarted
functions.description: Query whether search is started. A Boolean value that indicates whether the query has started.

parameters.name: Query
parameters.description: The reference number of the query.

functions.name: MetaDataQuery.IsStopped
functions.description: Query whether search is stopped. A Boolean value that indicates whether the query has stopped.

parameters.name: Query
parameters.description: The reference number of the query.

functions.name: MetaDataQuery.List
functions.description: Lists all IDs of metadata query objects. Returns IDs for all MetaDataQuery objects in memory. If this list is growing, you may have forgotten to call MetaDataQuery.Release in your scripts.

functions.name: MetaDataQuery.PathAtIndex
functions.description: Queries path of an item in results.

parameters.name: Query
parameters.description: The reference number of the query.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the item. Range from 0 to MetaDataQuery.ResultCount -1.

examples.label: Put paths in records:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 0 ]
Loop
New Record/Request
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "MetaDataQuery.PathAtIndex";
$Query
;
$Index
) ]
Set Field [
Search Applications::FilePath
;
$path
]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
# next
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
≥
$count
]
End Loop
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MetaDataQuery.PathAtIndex"; $Query; $Index ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$path </Calculation>
    <Field table="Search Applications" id="99999" name="FilePath "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index ≥ $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MetaDataQuery.SetQueryString
functions.description: Sets the predicate to search. The plugin will build the predicate with the given query string. Setting this property while a query is running stops the query and discards the current results. The receiver immediately starts a new query. See list of metadata keys here: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/coreservices/file_metadata/mditem/common_metadata_attribute_keys Expression syntax: https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/Carbon/Conceptual/SpotlightQuery/Concepts/QueryFormat.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40001849-CJBEJBHH

parameters.name: Query
parameters.description: The reference number of the query.

parameters.name: QueryString
parameters.description: The query string.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MetaDataQuery.SetQueryString"; $Query; "kMDItemContentType=\"com.apple.application-bundle\"")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Start a query:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$query
; Value:
MBS
( "
MetaDataQuery.Create
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MetaDataQuery.SetQueryString";
$Query
; "kMDItemContentType=\"com.apple.application-bundle\"") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:

functions.name: MetaDataQuery.SetScript
functions.description: Sets which script to call when initial gathering is done. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: ShellRef
parameters.description: The reference number from the shell.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

functions.name: MetaDataQuery.StopQuery
functions.description: Stops the receiver’s current query from gathering any further results. The receiver first completes gathering any unprocessed results. You call this function to stop a query that is generating too many results to be useful but you still want to access the available results. If the receiver is sent a startQuery message after performing this method, the existing results are discarded.

parameters.name: Query
parameters.description: The reference number of the query.

Due to the way the preferences work, we can read the value which the setting has, but we can only set the value for the next launch of the application.

functions.name: Preferences.GetValue
functions.description: Queries preferences value. Domain should be an unique name for your app, like with reverse domain name notation. An example:  "de.monkeybreadsoftware.filemaker.runtimes.MyApp". If you use no domain, the default one is used. If no value exists for the value, an empty text is returned. Values are read from registry on Windows and preference files on Mac. Key must not be an empty string.

parameters.name: Domain
parameters.description: The domain name for your preferences. Leave empty for default one.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The key for the value lookup.

functions.result: Returns the text for the preference value.

functions.name: Preferences.SetTextSelectionWithDragAndDrop
functions.description: Set the preference setting from FileMaker/Runtime about allowing text selection by drag and drop. Due to the way the preferences work, we can read the value which the setting has, but we can only set the value for the next launch of the application.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value: 0 for off and 1 for on.

functions.name: Preferences.SetValue
functions.description: Sets preferences value. Domain should be an unique name for your app, like with reverse domain name notation. An example:  "de.monkeybreadsoftware.filemaker.runtimes.MyApp". If you use no domain, the default one is used. Values are stored in registry on Windows and preference files on Mac. Key must not be an empty string. This is the location where you can store global settings like the last used username for login. Changing preferences of FileMaker only works, if you restart FileMaker for changes take effect. Going to the preferences dialog (even with cancel on exit), can cause FileMaker to write it's settings over what you just changed. To change windows preferences for Windows, you may better go with Registry functions.

parameters.name: Domain
parameters.description: The domain name for your preferences. Leave empty for default one.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The key for the value lookup.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value text.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The data type to use. (Mac only) Can be integer, double, boolean or text.

    </Value>

examples.label: Set option for custom user name in FileMaker preferences:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [

// read current options
~currentValue = MBS( "Preferences.GetValue"; ""; "Preferences:AppGenOptions");

// make sure 4th bit is set by setting bit for 8:
~newValue = MBS("Math.BitwiseOR"; ~currentValue; 8);

// and save back value
~result = MBS( "Preferences.SetValue"; ""; "Preferences:AppGenOptions"; ~newValue; "integer")

]; ~result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Switch off update checks:
examples.text_code:
Let(
[
oldValue =
MBS
( "
Preferences.GetValue
"; "com.filemaker.client.pro12"; "Preferences:AppGenOptions");
newValue =
MBS
("
Math.BitwiseOR
"; oldValue; 32 + 32768);
R =
MBS
( "Preferences.SetValue"; "com.filemaker.client.pro12"; "Preferences:AppGenOptions"; newValue; "integer")
];r )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
[
oldValue = MBS( "Preferences.GetValue"; "com.filemaker.client.pro12"; "Preferences:AppGenOptions");
newValue = MBS("Math.BitWiseOr"; oldValue; 32 + 32768);
R = MBS( "Preferences.SetValue"; "com.filemaker.client.pro12"; "Preferences:AppGenOptions"; newValue; "integer")
];r )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Screenshot
functions.description: Takes a screenshot of the screen indicated by the "ScreenID" parameter. Use "Set Field" to set the result of this function into any container field. Updated for macOS 10.7 in plugin version 2.5. Returns the image as container value, so you can assign it to a container field or save to disk with Container.WriteFile function. Added multi screen support for Windows with v11.3. See also Container.Screenshot to make screenshot of a layout object, WebView.Screenshot to make a screenshot of a web viewer and Window.Screenshot for a screenshot of a window.

parameters.name: ScreenID
parameters.description: Zero Based index of the screens. If blank, then 0 is assumed. Use in range from 0 to MBS( " Screen.Count " )-1.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: The format of the file (currently only JPEG is supported)

parameters.name: Quality
parameters.description: The quality of the image. From 0 to 100.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The desired file name. The image is not written to disk. This file name is just added to the container for later export.

functions.result: Returns the image as a container value so you can assign it to a container.

examples.label: Capture screen as JPEG:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
[
/*-----------------PARAMETERS--------------------*/ 
ScreenID = 0;
Format = "JPEG";
Quality = 90;
Filename = "MyCoolFile"
];
/*-------------------FUNCTION----------------------*/ 
MBS( 
"Screenshot";
ScreenID ;
Format ;
Quality ;
Filename 
)
)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Put screenshot in field:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$screenshot
; Value:
MBS
("Screenshot"; 0; "jpeg"; 90) ]
Set Field [
Contacts::Photo
Container ;
$screenshot
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Screenshot"; 0; "jpeg"; 90) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$screenshot</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$screenshot </Calculation>
    <Field table="Contacts" id="99999" name="Photo Container "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SharingService.GetEmailRecipients
functions.description: Queries current list of email recipients.

functions.name: SharingService.GetEmailSubject
functions.description: Queries current email subject.

functions.name: SharingService.ServicesForItems
functions.description: Queries available services for some items. Pass as many parameters as needed to include all URLs, native file paths, styled text or container values with images to the function as needed. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Item parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various flags. Pass 1 to include images for the sharing as base64 encoded PNGs, which you can show in the GUI.

parameters.name: Item...
parameters.description: Pass one parameter for each item. An item may be a container with an image, a list of URLs, a list of native file paths or styled text.

examples.label: Fill field with list of services available for items:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
("SharingService.ServicesForItems";
0; // flags, no images
// pass as many parameter with items. Empty ones will be ignore.
Sharing Services::URLs
;
Sharing Services::Text
;
Sharing Services::Image
;
Sharing Services::File
Paths) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
("
JSON.Colorize
";
$json
) ]
End If
Set Field [
Sharing Services::ServiceNames
;
$json
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SharingService.ServicesForItems";0; // flags, no images // pass as many parameter with items. Empty ones will be ignore. Sharing Services::URLs; Sharing Services::Text; Sharing Services::Image; Sharing Services::File Paths) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.Colorize"; $json) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$json </Calculation>
    <Field table="Sharing Services" id="99999" name="ServiceNames "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Value>

    </Repetition>

functions.name: SharingService.SetTrigger
functions.description: Sets a trigger to call for the share. This is called once after you call SharingService.ShareItems to report the result, but only if SharingService.ShareItems returns OK. You get a script parameter with a JSON containing a status key with the status, either success, cancel or failed. We include error dictionary with details on a failure and service key with details on the service.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

functions.name: SharingService.ShareHTML
functions.description: Shares html with services. You can let the user pick the service or pass it as service name. Pass as many parameters as needed to include native file paths, styled text or container values with images to the function as needed. Below a few sample service names, but there may be more installed on your computer.

parameters.name: ServiceName
parameters.description: The service name to use. Pass empty text to show menu at mouse cursor and let the user choose.

parameters.name: HTML
parameters.description: The HTML to parse into styled text to pass on.

parameters.name: Item...
parameters.description: Pass one parameter for each item. An item may be a container with an image, a list of URLs, a list of native file paths or styled text.

functions.name: SharingService.ShareItems
functions.description: Shares items with services. You can let the user pick the service or pass it as service name. Pass as many parameters as needed to include all URLs, native file paths, styled text or container values with images to the function as needed. Below a few sample service names, but there may be more installed on your computer.

parameters.name: ServiceName
parameters.description: The service name to use. Pass empty text to show menu at mouse cursor and let the user choose.

parameters.name: Item...
parameters.description: Pass one parameter for each item. An item may be a container with an image, a list of URLs, a list of native file paths or styled text.

examples.label: Share items:
examples.text_code:
# for the case you use email, we may predefine the subject & recipients we suggest
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SharingService.SetEmailSubject
";
Sharing Services::Email
Subject) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SharingService.SetEmailRecipients
";
Sharing Services::Email
Recipients) ]
#
# set trigger
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
SharingService.SetTrigger
"; Get(FileName); "Trigger") ]
#
# share them
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("SharingService.ShareItems";
Sharing Services::ServiceName
;
// pass as many parameter with items. Empty ones will be ignore.
Sharing Services::URLs
;
Sharing Services::Text
;
Sharing Services::Image
;
Sharing Services::File
Paths) ]
Set Field [
Sharing Services::Result
;
$r
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> for the case you use email, we may predefine the subject & recipients we suggest</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SharingService.SetEmailSubject"; Sharing Services::Email Subject) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SharingService.SetEmailRecipients"; Sharing Services::Email Recipients) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set trigger</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SharingService.SetTrigger"; Get(FileName); "Trigger") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> share them</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SharingService.ShareItems"; Sharing Services::ServiceName;// pass as many parameter with items. Empty ones will be ignore. Sharing Services::URLs; Sharing Services::Text; Sharing Services::Image; Sharing Services::File Paths) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    <Field table="Sharing Services" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.AddLineNumbers
functions.description: Adds line numbers to text. Preserves the styles in the text and adds line numbers as prefix. Depending on how many lines we have, we may use 2 to 6 digits for numbers. Works fine for JSON, XML, JavaScript, HTML or any other text. See also XML.Format & JSON.Format to format and XML.Colorize & JSON.Colorize for colors.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to process.

parameters.name: StyleTemplate
parameters.description: The styled text to use as template for line numbers. We only use style for the nubers and text is ignored. If empty, this parameter is ignored.

parameters.name: Delimiter
parameters.description: The optional delimiter. Default are two space characters.

parameters.name: Prefix
parameters.description: The prefix to put before the number.

examples.label: Format, colorize and apply line numbers:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.AddLineNumbers"; 
	// colorize it
	MBS("JSON.Colorize"; 
	  	// format before colorize
		MBS( "JSON.Format"; JSON Input)
	); 
	// define which style to use for numbers
	TextColor ( 
		TextFont ( " "; "Courier" ); 
		RGB ( 0 ; 0 ; 255 ) 
	)
)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Add <> around numbers:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.AddLineNumbers"; "Hello¶World¶Test"; ""; "> "; "<" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.AppendTextFile
functions.description: Appends a text file with the given text. You have to decide which encoding to use (if not native). We support a few encodings, but we can add more if you need something special. If the file does not exist, it will be created. You can use this function for logging to a text file. Please use Text.ReplaceNewLine to define which line endings the file has. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to save.

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: The native file path. Something like "/Users/cs/desktop/test.txt" on Mac and "C:\Programs\Data\test.txt" on Windows. Files without path end in the root directory on Mac.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text parameter. Default is native. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: Write UTF-8 file with some text:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Text.AppendTextFile"; $Text; $Path; "utf8" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Write a test text file:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.CapitalCase
functions.description: Converts text to capital case letters. First letter of a word is made upper case, all others to lower case. Often people enter in forms all kinds of upper/lower case letters and you may want to normalize using this function. See also Text.UpperCase , Text.LowerCase and Text.RemoveAccents . This function works on pure  text, so any text style is lost.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to process.

functions.name: Text.Character
functions.description: Creates a character with given number. The FileMaker Char() function works only for unicode values up to 65535. This function works with higher values up to 2 billion. See also Text.Code .

parameters.name: Character Number
parameters.description: The unicode codepoint.

examples.label: Test the function:
examples.text_code:
"Shows a musical symbol: " & char(119070) &" " &
MBS
("Text.Character"; 119070)
Example result:
Shows a musical symbol: 䩾 𝄞
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Shows a musical symbol: " & char(119070) &" " & MBS("Text.Character"; 119070)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Get smiley:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Text.Character"; 128512)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Build country flag from country code:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Text.Character"; 127397 + Code ( "D" )) &
MBS
( "Text.Character"; 127397 + Code ( "E" ))
Example result:
🇩🇪
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.Character"; 127397 + Code ( "D" )) & MBS( "Text.Character"; 127397 + Code ( "E" ))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.Characters
functions.description: Returns the character codes. Similar to walking over all characters, getting the unicode code point for the character and return its number. This shows UTF-32 characters.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to process.

examples.label: Get characters from Hello:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.Characters"; "Hello" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Show characters for emoji:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.Characters"; "🍎" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.Code
functions.description: Queries the number of a character. Returns the unicode code point. Compared to FileMaker's Code() function, this function does handle codes over 65535. See also Text.Character .

parameters.name: Char
parameters.description: The text character to look at.

examples.label: Query code for a smiley:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.Code"; "😀" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.Concat
functions.description: Creates a text with concatenating all the given parameters. Pass as many parameters as needed. See also Text.List to build a list. This function works on styled text. If you need a delimiter, please check Text.ConcatWithDelimiter function. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Text parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: Text...
parameters.description: The text to add.

examples.label: Joins texts:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Text.Concat"; "apple"; ""; "banana")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Concat texts:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.Concat"; "🍎"; " "; "🍓"; " "; "🍒" ) )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.ConcatWithDelimiter
functions.description: Creates a text with concatenating all the given parameters. Pass as many parameters as needed. See also Text.List to build a list. This function works on styled text. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Text parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: Delimiter
parameters.description: The delimiter to use.

parameters.name: Text...
parameters.description: The text to add.

examples.label: Concat with empty texts:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.ConcatWithDelimiter"; " - "; "Vorname"; ""; "Nachname"; "" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

functions.name: Text.ConvertToTextEncoding
functions.description: Converts text into a text encoding. Returns text in hex encoded bytes. As we use iconv library here, we can handle many more encodings than the standard FileMaker text functions used within the plugin. If the string //IGNORE is appended to to-encoding, characters that cannot be converted are discarded and an error is printed after conversion. If the string //TRANSLIT is appended to to-encoding, characters being converted are transliterated when needed and possible.  This means that when a character cannot be represented in the target character set, it can be approximated through one or several similar looking characters.  Characters that are outside of the target character set and cannot be transliterated are replaced with a question mark (?) in the output. Encoding list with alternative names: ANSI_X3.4-1968,ANSI_X3.4-1986,ASCII,CP367,IBM367,ISO-IR-6,ISO646-US,ISO_646.IRV:1991,US,US-ASCII,CSASCII UTF-8 ISO-10646-UCS-2,UCS-2,CSUNICODE ( "Text.ConvertToTextEncoding"; "Hello World äöü"; "UTF-8" ))

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

functions.name: Text.ConvertUnicodeToCharacterComposition
functions.description: Converts text by applying unicode character normalization. This function replaces character represented by decomposed representation with the composed representation. see also https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unicode_equivalence Rewritten for version 12.1 to preserve text styles and be quicker.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to process.

examples.label: Test function:
examples.text_code:
#some text
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:"ä ö ü"]
#now make decomposed
Set Variable [
$decomposedText
; Value:
MBS
("
Text.ConvertUnicodeToCharacterDecomposition
";
$text
)]
#and composed again
Set Variable [
$composedText
; Value:
MBS
("Text.ConvertUnicodeToCharacterComposition";
$decomposedText
)]
#Now show results. See the 776 for the Diaeresis?
Show Custom Dialog ["Result";
$text
& "  " &
MBS
("
Text.Characters
";
$text
) & ¶  &
$decomposedText
& "  " &
MBS
("
Text.Characters
…]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>some text</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"ä ö ü"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>now make decomposed</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Text.ConvertUnicodeToCharacterDecomposition"; $text)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$decomposedText</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>and composed again</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Text.ConvertUnicodeToCharacterComposition"; $decomposedText)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$composedText</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Now show results. See the 776 for the Diaeresis?</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Result"; $text & "  " & MBS("Text.Characters"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$text) & ¶  &   $decomposedText & "  " & MBS("Text.Characters…</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.ConvertUnicodeToCharacterDecomposition

parameters.description: The text to process.

functions.name: Text.CountWords
functions.description: Counts words. We split the text into words and then count for each how often it happens. The result contains count, tab and the word and is not sorted.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to count words.

examples.label: Count words:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Text.CountWords"; "Hello World Hello" )
Example result:
1   World
2   Hello
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.CountWords"; "Hello World Hello" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Works with umlauts:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Text.CountWords"; "ä ö ü ä ö ö ö ö ß ß" )
Example result:
2   ß
2   ä
5   ö
1   ü
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.CountWords"; "ä ö ü ä ö ö ö ö ß ß" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.DecodeBytesFromBase64
functions.description: Decodes base64 string and encodes it as byte values.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to process.

functions.name: Text.DecodeFromBase64
functions.description: Decode a base 64 string. This is for text only. Data encoded as base64 would probably be truncated as FileMaker texts do not contain NULL bytes.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to process.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is native. This function can also handle UTF-16 as well as UTF-16LE and UTF-16BE for little/big endian byte order. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Calculate a JSON Web Token (JWT) in FileMaker:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
secret = "secretkey";
header = JWT RS256::Header;
payload = JWT RS256::Payload;
encodedString = MBS( "Text.EncodeToBase64URL"; header; "UTF-8" ) & "." & MBS( "Text.EncodeToBase64URL"; payload; "UTF-8" );

// calculate hash
hash = MBS( "RSA.Sign"; "SHA256"; 
"Text"; encodedString; "UTF-8"; 
"Text"; JWT RS256::Key; "UTF-8"; 
"text"; JWT RS256::Password; "UTF-8"; 
"base64url"; "");

// and built final result:
result = encodedString & "." & hash
]; result )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$EncodedFileName</Name>
  </Step>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.FilterASCII
functions.description: Filters text to ASCII. Removes all non ASCII characters which can't fit into 7bit. This function removes those for software, which may not handle them correctly. We convert 23 variants of curly quotes into ASCII quotes. See also Text.RemoveControlCharacters and Text.RemoveAccents functions.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to process.

parameters.name: Replacement
parameters.description: Replacement text for invalid characters. Default is empty.

Example result:

functions.name: Text.FilterSwissQRCode
functions.description: Filters characters for Swiss QR code for payments. We pass through allowed characters: Basic-Latin (Unicodepoint U+0020 - U+007E) Latin1-Supplement (Unicodepoint U+00A0 - U+00FF) Latin Extended-A (Unicodepoint U+0100 - U+017F) And these individual characters: Ș - (LATIN CAPITAL LETTER S WITH COMMA BELOW, Unicodepoint U+0218) ș - (LATIN SMALL LETTER S WITH COMMA BELOW, Unicodepoint U+0219) Ț - (LATIN CAPITAL LETTER T WITH COMMA BELOW, Unicodepoint U+021A) ț - (LATIN SMALL LETTER T WITH COMMA BELOW, Unicodepoint U+021B) € - (EURO SIGN, Unicodepoint U+20AC) For other characters, we try to translate them to allowed characters, e.g ¼ is changed to 1/4.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to filter.

parameters.name: MultiLine
parameters.description: Pass 1 to enable newline characters. If enabled, we allow Char(13) to go through. Other new line characters get converted to Char(13). Default is 0 to disable newlines and work on values only.

examples.label: Filter some text:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Text.FilterSwissQRCode"; "Hello World ≤ «∑€®†Ω 日本国" )
Example result:
Hello World <= «€®
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.FilterSwissQRCode"; "Hello World ≤ «∑€®†Ω 日本国" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.description: Filters text to UTF-16. Removes all UTF-32 characters which can't fit into UTF-16. Normally UTF-32 range characters exceeding the UTF-16 range are stored as two characters in UTF-16. This function removes those for software, which may not handle them correctly.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to process.

parameters.name: Replacement
parameters.description: Replacement text for invalid characters. Default is empty.

functions.name: Text.FindBetween
functions.signature: MBS( "Text.FindBetween"; Text; StartTag; EndTag { ; IgnoreCase; NthItem } )
functions.description: Searches text for text between the two search strings. This function is useful for quickly scanning XML documents. For more complex XML queries, please use XML.Query function.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to search inside.

parameters.name: StartTag
parameters.description: The first search text, e.g. start tag.

parameters.name: EndTag
parameters.description: The end tag to search, e.g. second search text.

parameters.name: IgnoreCase
parameters.description: Optional, whether to ignore case in search. Default is 0 for case sensitive search.

parameters.name: NthItem
parameters.description: How many items to ignore before doing real search. Pass 1 to get second item.

functions.name: Text.FromStyles
functions.description: Creates styled text from JSON made with Text.Style. Great function to construct styled text via JSON functions. Input is a JSON array with objects. Each object with a "text" item is considered and others ignored. If there is a "hasSize" entry with false, we ignore size. Otherwise we read "size" value and use it. If there is a "hasFont" entry with false, we ignore font details. Otherwise we lookup "font" entry and use it as font name. If that is missing, we look for "fontID" entry with a font ID. For FileMaker before version 14, we look also for "fontScript" entry. If there is a "hasColor" entry with false, we ignore color details. Otherwise we look for "red", "green" and "blue". If one exists, we use them for color. If an "alpha" entry exists, we add the alpha value. Alternatively we use "colorHex" for a hex color value. If there is a "hasFace" entry with false, we ignore face style. Otherwise we lookup "face" with a numeric value for the style. We then also look for boolean entries named bold, italic, underline, outline, shadow, condense, extend, strikethrough, smallCaps, superscript, subscript, wordUnderline, doubleUnderline entries to adjust style.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

examples.label: Create styled text with JSON:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.FromStyles"; "[{\"text\":\"Hello\", \"underlined\": true}, {\"text\": \" \"}, {\"text\":\"World\", \"strikethrough\": true}]")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.HTMLtoJSON
functions.description: Parses HTML and transforms to JSON. Sets error log, which can be queried with Text.TidyErrorLog function. For options, check this website: https://api.html-tidy.org/tidy/tidylib_api_5.8.0/tidy_quickref.html Use JSON.Colorize to apply colors if needed. By default we apply options to use utf-8 for input and output as encoding. Line endings use Char(13) as FileMaker does and we disable the tidy mark for the html header.

parameters.name: Input
parameters.description: The input HTML.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The tidy options as JSON object.

functions.name: Text.HTMLtoStyledText
functions.description: Converts HTML to styled text. This uses tidy to parse the HTML and then convert the nodes to styled text for FileMaker. This way you can preserve bold, italic and other styles as much as possible. Does not read style definitions, but only inline styles. Sets error log, which can be queried with Text.TidyErrorLog function. For options, check this website: https://api.html-tidy.org/tidy/tidylib_api_5.8.0/tidy_quickref.html We support the following html tags: B or STRONG for bold I or EM for italic U for underline S for strikethrough SUB for subscript SUP for superscript FONT with attributes for face, size and color. Color with # hex notation. SPAN with CSS styles UL and OL for lists with LI for elements. BLOCKQUOTE and PRE for passing through text. A for links where we print link in [] IMG for images where we print alt text and reference in [] H1 to H6 for headers with font size 14 to 24. For CSS we do font-weight with bold, font-style with italic, text-decoration with underline and line-through, color with hex notation, font-size and font-family. This is a limited implementation aimed to handle the output of our Markdown functions and to work without a Webviewer.

parameters.name: HTML
parameters.description: The html input.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The tidy options as JSON object.

examples.label: Convert HTML to styled text:
examples.text_code:
Set Field [
MarkDown::StyledText
;
MBS
("Text.HTMLtoStyledText";
MarkDown::HTML
) ]
# show in webviewer to compare
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WebView.LoadHTML
"; "web";
MarkDown::HTML
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("Text.HTMLtoStyledText"; MarkDown::HTML) </Calculation>
    <Field table="MarkDown" id="99999" name="StyledText "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> show in webviewer to compare</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.LoadHTML"; "web"; MarkDown::HTML ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [
$id

; Value:

("

";

";
$id

$id

& ¶ & "</body>" & ¶ & "</html>" ]

$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WebView.LoadHTML
"; "web";
MarkDown::HTML
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("MarkDown.Create"; MarkDown::InputMarkDown) </Calculation>

      </Button>

    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

    </Repetition>

functions.name: Text.IdentifyData
functions.description: Checks file type from the data. We read the first bytes of the file and decide which file type is may be. You may use "Hex" or "base64" as encoding to check type before decoding it. Supported types:

parameters.name: Text

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text parameter. Default is UTF-8 for version. Can also be "hex" or "base64" to provide data encoded with hex or base64 algorithm.

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.InvalidCharactersForEncoding
functions.description: Looks for invalid characters for an encoding. Checks the text to return all characters which are not preserved when converting text to given text encoding. Returns an empty text if all characters are supported in given encoding. The returned text should have no duplicates, so each character shows only once in the order of first appearance. Some characters may not be directly supported, but still convert to similar looking characters.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to process.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for the resulting string. Default is native. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: Check if checkmark is allowed in Latin1:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Checks for characters:

    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.JaroWinklerDistance
functions.description: Calculates the string distance. Value is normalized, so 0.0 is equal text and 1.0 is totally unequal. see also http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaro-Winkler_distance

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: First text string.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: Second text string.

examples.label: Calculate difference between two strings:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.JaroWinklerDistance"; "Hello"; "Hallo" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.LeftTrim
functions.description: Trims whitespace on the left side. May remove low ASCII characters like tab, CR, LF and space. Also removes alt-space and delete character. This function works on pure text, so any text style is lost.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to trim.

examples.label: Trim hello:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.LeftTrim"; " ¶ Hello ¶ ")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.Length
functions.description: Query length of text. This is number of UTF16 code points. Some characters need 2 code points.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to check.

examples.label: Measure length of emoji:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.Length"; "🍎" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.LevenshteinDistance
functions.description: Calculates the string distance. Value is normalized, so 0.0 is equal text and 1.0 is totally unequal, but value can be higher than 1.0 if text length is very different. see also http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Levenshtein_distance

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: First text string.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: Second text string.

examples.label: Calculate difference between two strings:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.LevenshteinDistance"; "Hello"; "Hallo" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.List
functions.description: Creates a list with all the given parameters. Pass as many parameters as needed. Can include empty parameters. We put in a Char(13) between values to form a list. This function works on pure  text, so any text style is lost. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Text parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: Text...
parameters.description: The text to add.

examples.label: Joins text including empty values:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Text.List"; "apple"; ""; "banana")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.ListWords
functions.description: Split words and list them. Words are listed in same order. We find words based on alpha and numeric characters and use all others as separator. You can use List.CountValues to count the values in the list or directly use Text.CountWords . This function works on pure  text, so any text style is lost.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to split.

examples.label: Split words:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Text.ListWords"; "Hello World,äöü." )
Example result:
Hello
World
äöü
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.ListWords"; "Hello World,äöü." )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.LowerCase
functions.description: Converts text to lower case letters. See also Text.UpperCase , Text.CapitalCase and Text.RemoveAccents . This function works on pure  text, so any text style is lost.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to process.

examples.label: Lower case of Hamburg:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Test accented characters:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.MergeFields
functions.description: Merge field and variable names into text. This functions look for items between << and >> to replace those with real values from fields, variables or expressions. This function does not preserve formatting. For use in XML or HTML, we can encode all replacement values for you. If you allow expressions, that may include plugins calls, so user could do anything via MBS calls!

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The input text.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The various flags. 1 to allow field names. 2 to allow variables. 4 to allow expressions to evaluate. 16 to output XML 32 to output HTML.

examples.label: Use function with Variable:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$FirstName
; Value: "Christian" ]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value: "Hello <<
$FirstName
>>" ]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
MBS
( "Text.MergeFields";
$text
; 1+2 ) ]
Show Custom Dialog [
$text
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Christian" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$FirstName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Hello <<$FirstName>>" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.MergeFields"; $text; 1+2 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> $text </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.MimeTypeToFileExtension
functions.description: Returns file extension for given mime type. Extension is returned with dot prefix. Please report any missing file extensions in our lookup table, so we can add more. See also Text.FileExtensionToMimeType .

parameters.name: MimeType
parameters.description: The mime type to lookup.

examples.label: Checks for PDF:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.RTFToText
functions.description: Parses RTF to styled text. Should find texts, font name/size, some styles like italic/bold/underline and color. We read the input with Windows ANSI encoding to get characters right for RTF files from Windows without encoding specs, so if you read it from a file with Text.ReadTextFile , please specify "Windows" as encoding to use. If the container contains a RTF file, you may need to read text content with Text.ReadTextFromContainer first.

parameters.name: RTFData
parameters.description: The RTF data text.

examples.label: Convert RTF in a field to styled text in a new field
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "Text.RTFToText"; test::RTFText ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="test" id="99999" name="StypedText "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

containerValue =
Kontakte::Foto
;
plist =
MBS
( "
PList.ReadData
"; containerValue );

text =

( "Text.RTFToText"; rtf)

  containerValue = Kontakte::Foto;

functions.name: Text.ReadEMLXFile
functions.description: Reads email text from emlx file. This function provides the raw text from an emlx file as Apple Mail uses them to store emails. You may need to use Text.ReplaceNewLine function to change new line characters. You have to decide which encoding to use (if not native). We support a few encodings, but we can add more if you need something special. See also Text.ReadTextFile for normal text files and eml files.

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: The native file path. Something like "/Users/cs/desktop/test.txt" on Mac and "C:\Programs\Data\test.txt" on Windows. Files without path end in the root directory on Mac.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is native. This function can also handle UTF-16 as well as UTF-16LE and UTF-16BE for little/big endian byte order. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.result: Returns text on success and error on failure.

examples.label: Read email file:
examples.text_code:
# emlx test in file Contacts
Set Variable [
$path
; Value: "/Users/cs/Desktop/28213.emlx" ]
Set Variable [
$emailSourceText
; Value:
MBS
( "Text.ReadEMLXFile";
$path
; "UTF-8") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Variable [
$email
; Value:
MBS
( "
EmailParser.Parse
";
$EmailSourceText
; "UTF8") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Email subject" ;
MBS
( "
EmailParser.Subject
";
$email
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
EmailParser.Release
";
$email
) ]
End If
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> emlx test in file Contacts</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"/Users/cs/Desktop/28213.emlx" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.ReadEMLXFile"; $path; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$emailSourceText</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "EmailParser.Parse"; $EmailSourceText; "UTF8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$email</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Email subject" ; MBS( "EmailParser.Subject"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$email ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">

    </Value>

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Text.ReadTextFile
functions.description: Reads a text file. You have to decide which encoding to use (if not native). We support a few encodings, but we can add more if you need something special. Some characters will be wrong if the encoding is not correct. See also Files.ReadJPEG , Files.ReadPDF , Files.ReadPNG , Files.ReadFile and RichText.ReadFile . This function reads only up to 1 GB of text. And even than a 32-bit FileMaker may fail to allocate enough memory to handle the text. When passing UTF-16 as encoding, we can detect byte order if there is a Byte Order Mark. Use Container.GetText function to read text content from a file in container. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: The native file path. Something like "/Users/cs/desktop/test.txt" on Mac and "C:\Programs\Data\test.txt" on Windows. Files without path end in the root directory on Mac.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is native. This function can also handle UTF-16 as well as UTF-16LE and UTF-16BE for little/big endian byte order. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.result: Returns text on success and error on failure.

examples.label: Read system.log file:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Text.ReadTextFile"; "/var/log/system.log"; "utf8")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Read Windows ANSI XML:

$text
; Value:

("Text.ReadTextFile"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.xml"; "Windows")]
# remove the encoding hint:
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:Substitute(
$text
; "<?xml version=\"
1.0
\" encoding=\"WINDOWS-1252\"?>"; "")]
# now xml is unicode text and can be passed to
XML
functions.
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> read and convert to from Windows ANSI to unicode</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Text.ReadTextFile"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.xml"; "Windows")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> remove the encoding hint:</Text>
  </Step>

      <Calculation>Substitute($text; "<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"WINDOWS-1252\"?>"; "")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now xml is unicode text and can be passed to XML functions.</Text>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>
